data.title: Rubicon Organics Launches 1964 Supply Co.™ in the U.K.
data.description: A corporation has introduced its 1964 Supply Co.™ range of medical cannabis items in the United Kingdom by collaborating with 4C LABS for distribution.

data.title: SpaceX's IPO worth $1.75T nears, Bitget offers pre-IPO exposure with new platform, Republic-issued token.
data.description: Bitget has recently launched its IPO Prime platform, which features a token offering tied to SpaceX's future performance in the public market. This move allows investors to gain exposure to SpaceX's performance when it eventually goes public. It marks an innovative development in the cryptocurrency market and could potentially attract a significant number of investors.

data.title: CIA used AI for its first intel report and plans to have AI teams.
data.description: Leaders at the CIA have confirmed that the agency has employed artificial intelligence to create its inaugural self-directed intelligence report. The agency anticipates utilizing complete AI agent groups moving forward.

data.title: ServiceNow Stock Craters 19% In Week's Worst S&P 500 Showing
data.description: ServiceNow Inc. (NYSE: NOW) is experiencing its worst week in ten years, with a 19% drop in stock price cementing its position as the poorest performer in the S&P 500 during this period. This decline marks the sharpest weekly decrease for NOW since 2016, signaling a significant shift for a stock that was previously considered a top choice for investing in digital transformation and enterprise AI. The plummet in NOW's stock price is attributable to the overall downgrade of high-multiple software companies due to concerns about AI potentially reducing pricing power in traditional SaaS sectors. Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) and Anthropic unveiled new models, while Michael Burry also contributed bearish comments, particularly targeting Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR). Notably, this negative sentiment is not limited to only these companies, but has impacted... You can find the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: xAI, led by Elon Musk, sues Colorado over new high-risk AI law, amid increasing legal battles surrounding its Grok chatbot.
data.description: xAI, a company owned by Elon Musk, is opposing Colorado's recently implemented high-risk AI legislation. This move comes as part of the increasing legal scrutiny surrounding its Grok chatbot. The challenge highlights the pushback against the law and raises questions about the implications for AI technology. The situation underscores the complexities surrounding the regulation of AI, with xAI being at the center of this legal debate. The company's stance adds to the ongoing discussions regarding the use of AI and the need for clear regulations in the industry. This development could potentially have far-reaching consequences for the future of AI in the state of Colorado and beyond.

data.title: Zcash may hit $420 post 62% surge, surpassing Bitcoin. Traders foresee further growth for privacy-centric coin ZEC.
data.description: Zcash, a coin focused on privacy, has seen a significant surge surpassing Bitcoin. Many analysts believe that the price of ZEC is poised to continue increasing after a substantial rise.

data.title: Officials caution banks about cybersecurity threats linked to Anthropic's Mythos AI; Treasury and Federal Reserve issue warning.
data.description: Officials from the Treasury and Federal Reserve recently warned banks about potential cybersecurity threats associated with Anthropic’s cutting-edge Mythos AI model. The advanced AI technology poses a significant risk to banks, prompting the alert to be issued. The alert highlighted the specific vulnerabilities that could be exploited by cybercriminals, emphasizing the urgent need for enhanced security measures. This warning comes at a crucial time, with cybersecurity incidents on the rise globally. It is imperative for banks to take proactive steps to protect their systems and customer data from potential breaches. The collaboration between government agencies and financial institutions is vital in addressing these emerging threats and safeguarding the integrity of the banking sector.

data.title: Musk's SpaceX Reportedly Suffered $5 Billion Loss Last Year Despite High Revenue
data.description: SpaceX, led by Elon Musk, reportedly faced a loss of nearly $5 billion last year, despite generating more than $18.5 billion in revenue. This financial result includes the performance of xAI, an AI firm founded by Musk and bought by SpaceX in February 2026. The combined financial data for SpaceX and xAI was recently revealed, showing a significant difference from earlier reports by Reuters in January, which highlighted profits of over $8 billion on revenues of $15-16 billion. SpaceX has not yet commented on this new development. An in-depth analysis of this news story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Hong Kong Greenlights Stablecoins: HSBC, StanChart JV Win First Licenses
data.description: HSBC Holdings plc (NYSE: HSBC) saw a slight increase in its shares after Hong Kong regulators recently approved new stablecoin issuer licenses. This development signals progress in overseeing digital assets, as the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) granted licenses to Anchorpoint Financial Limited, a joint venture between Standard Chartered PLC (OTC: SCBFF) and The Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation Limited. The regulatory framework in Hong Kong aims to formalize stablecoin operations, with a focus on balancing innovation with ensuring user and market safeguards. This significant step showcases the authorities' efforts to regulate digital assets effectively.

data.title: Brian Armstrong Killed The Clarity Act In January — But Now He's Changed His Mind
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong recently expressed his support for the Clarity Act on April 10, following his previous opposition to the bill back in January. The new compromise now permits activity-based stablecoin rewards while prohibiting passive yield. Armstrong's initial rejection of the bill in January had caused significant turmoil within the crypto industry, leading to a delay in the Senate Banking Committee's scheduled markup. This decision faced criticism from various leaders, including Chris Dixon from a16z crypto, who urged for the bill's progression. Over the following months, Senators Thom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks reached a compromise, reaching a tentative agreement on stablecoin yield by late March. The White House Council of Economic Advisers also released a report on April 8, emphasizing the importance of the legislation.

data.title: Anthony Pompliano Says Bitcoin Has 'True Product-Market Fit'
data.description: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is increasingly playing a significant role in global geopolitics and financial markets. This was highlighted when Iran reportedly proposed a ceasefire agreement that included a $1 per barrel transit tax to be paid in BTC. In a recent podcast episode, Anthony Pompliano emphasized the importance of Bitcoin as a digital, decentralized, and non-sovereign asset, stating that it is showing "true product-market fit." Pompliano noted the growing interest in Bitcoin from traditional finance and geopolitical players. He also mentioned the launch of a low-fee Bitcoin exchange-traded fund by Morgan Stanley as a sign of increasing institutional adoption. The use of Bitcoin in geopolitical agreements signals its expanding influence beyond just the financial sector.

data.title: Palantir Sell-Off 'Way Over-Blown'—Wall Street Bull Sees 70% Upside For Stock
data.description: The stock of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) had a rough week, experiencing a significant sell-off. However, a prominent Wall Street analyst believes the sell-off is exaggerated and sees a potential upside of around 70% from current levels. The downturn in PLTR intensified after investor Michael Burry criticized the company in a now-deleted post, claiming that Anthropic is outperforming Palantir by capturing a large portion of the new enterprise AI market. Burry stated that Anthropic's approach is more accessible and cost-effective compared to Palantir's complex software. This criticism led to a decline of over 6% on Wednesday and more than 7% on Thursday, contributing to a year-to-date decrease of almost 30% from the stock's peak value. On the contrary, Wedbush tech analyst Dan Ives remains optimistic about Palantir's outlook. He believes that the threat posed by Anthropic to PLTR is overstated. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Closes Higher Ahead Of Peace Talks; Palantir Plunges (Live Coverage)
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones experienced a slight decrease in a day of mixed trading. Notably, chipmaker TSMC, associated with Nvidia, saw a significant rise in its stock value due to strong sales. On the other hand, the Nasdaq ended the day on a positive note before upcoming peace talks. Meanwhile, Palantir faced a sharp decline in its stock price. The day's market performances were closely monitored by investors.

data.title: New 'Space Invaders' inspired game offers real Bitcoin rewards for skilled, lucky, or rich players. Free-to-play with high difficulty.
data.description: A recently launched online game inspired by a retro arcade favorite is now available for free, offering players the opportunity to earn actual Bitcoin. However, achieving this reward will prove to be quite challenging.

data.title: 20-year-old man arrested for throwing Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's SF home; threatened to burn down OpenAI HQ.
data.description: Authorities have reported that a male aged 20 is said to have made threats to set fire to the headquarters of OpenAI following a recent incident.

data.title: Survey: Gen Z increasingly uses AI despite declining enthusiasm, with fewer feeling hopeful about the technology.
data.description: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) among Generation Z has increased significantly, with a notable rise in adoption rates. However, feelings of enthusiasm towards this technology have decreased considerably, along with a decline in sentiments of optimism.
